close_reason: run_on_new_thread semantics are documented at the call sites. Adding trait-level docs about internal helpers adds maintenance burden. The helper is pub(crate)-level utility, not a user-facing API.
description: run_on_new_thread in secretx-core/src/lib.rs is exposed as pub under #[cfg(feature = "blocking")]. The function spawns a scoped OS thread with a current_thread Tokio runtime to run an async future synchronously. Its doc comment (on get_blocking) mentions task-dropping behavior, but run_on_new_thread itself has no doc comment explaining: (1) that the returned value is the result of driving the future to completion, (2) that any futures awaited inside that are not yet polled when the runtime shuts down may be silently dropped, (3) that this is intended for FFI/blocking callsites only and is not a general async-to-sync bridge. Callers who find run_on_new_thread in scope may use it for arbitrary futures without understanding these constraints. What goes wrong: a caller passes a future that spawns sub-tasks (tokio::spawn) inside the single-threaded runtime; those tasks are silently dropped when the outer future completes and the runtime shuts down. Fix: add a complete doc comment to run_on_new_thread covering the single-thread-runtime constraint, task-drop semantics, and intended use case.
title: run_on_new_thread is pub but its task-drop semantics are undocumented
